title: loranthus incanus var. gossweileri
intro: Loranthus incanus var. gossweileri is a plant species commonly known as the white mistletoe, a hemiparasitic plant that obtains some of its nutrients by attaching to the branches of its host tree. The white mistletoe has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral activities. The health benefits of Loranthus incanus var. gossweileri include its potential to reduce fever, relieve respiratory issues, and treat various skin conditions, such as wounds and ulcers. Herbal preparations of the white mistletoe are typically made by infusing the leaves or stems in hot water to create a tea, or by drying and powdering the plant material for use in capsules or tinctures.
